item.title: State and territory ministers react to the Albanese government's NDIS changes
item.description: State leaders are reacting to the sweeping cuts coming for the National Disability Insurance Scheme, with one minister arguing the federal government will "walk away" from those in need.

item.title: Michaela Leonard to become Wallaroos' most-capped player in Anzac Day Test against New Zealand
item.description: Rugby was not on Michaela Leonard's radar when she was a child in Canberra, but she is about to achieve a historic milestone when the Wallaroos meet the Black Ferns on Anzac Day.

item.title: Indonesia's finance minister suggests Strait of Malacca toll before immediately backtracking
item.description: The suggestion of levying ships using the Strait of Malacca was suggested and then walked back by Indonesia's finance minister, but has prompted a response from Canberra.

item.title: Keiron Beau James Hedges charged with threatening to 'blow up' Perth Airport terminal
item.description: A man who allegedly threatened to "blow up" Perth Airport's main international terminal, forcing its evacuation and causing traveller chaos, has appeared in court.

item.title: James Valentine made radio look easy. He was brave and generous on air and off
item.description: Children grew up with him, adults grew old with him, he kept us connected to our city, the world and each other. James helped us make sense of life and gave us permission to laugh at the absurdity of it all — and ourselves.

item.title: Bulldogs star cleared of serious neck injury after frightening fall
item.description: The Western Bulldogs receive positive news from the scans on Aaron Naughton's neck after a frightening fall in Thursday night's loss to Sydney.

item.title: Canavan's campaigning in Farrer proves a frosty foray
item.description: This week the Albanese government pushed out a major reform ahead of the federal budget, the Nationals leader was out in the cold on the campaign trail and a former Treasury secretary had some choice words about the push for gas tax reform.

item.title: Russell Brand admits to having sex with 16-year-old when he was 30
item.description: British actor and comedian Russell Brand, who is facing multiple sexual assault charges, has admitted to having sex with a 16-year-old when he was 30.

item.title: David Malouf, esteemed Australian author, poet, essayist and librettist, dies aged 92
item.description: Erudite and urbane, David Malouf — perhaps most famous for his portrait of mid-century Brisbane in his 1975 novel Johnno — will be fondly remembered for his immeasurable contribution to Australian letters.

item.title: Grapevine berry inner necrosis virus officially detected for first time in multiple Australian states
item.description: A grapevine virus known in China and Japan that affects plant growth and productivity has been detected in Australia for the first time in Victorian, Queensland and South Australian table grapes.

item.title: Albanese poised to kill off move to increase taxes on gas giants
item.description: The government will not increase taxes on gas giants in next month's budget, echoing the industry's argument that exporters are already paying tens of billions of dollars in taxes each year.

item.title: 'Unusual' Adelaide warm weather sees residents soak up sun at beach
item.description: Adelaide residents have been making the most of unusually warm weather for the end of April, with crowds flocking to the beach before the school holidays finish.

item.title: How the new eligibility test for NDIS participants could work
item.description: Labor's plan to create a new "assessment tool" to determine future NDIS eligibility based on functional need could backfire without the right settings, say experts.

item.title: Dingo attacks on three children in WA's Karijini National Park prompt warning
item.description: Three children have been attacked by dingoes in Karijini National Park since Saturday, prompting a warning from authorities.

item.title: Kyle Sandilands and Jackie O counter-sued by radio show broadcaster
item.description: The company that broadcast the Kyle and Jackie O Show has launched a counter claim against the duo seeking damages for breach of contract, losses in profit and advertising revenue, and legal costs.

item.title: Thursday finance with Daniel Ziffer
item.description: Latest trusted news from the world of finance and business.

item.title: Marshall fumes at NRL refs as Koroisau faces hip-drop tackle verdict
item.description: Wests Tigers coach Benji Marshall has launched a passionate criticism of NRL officiating, following his side's 33-14 win over the Canberra Raiders.

item.title: Lance Collard's 'crippling' ban reduced to four weeks with two suspended after St Kilda appeal
item.description: St Kilda successfully appeals the AFL's nine-week ban handed to forward Lance Collard over a homophobic slur, reducing it to four weeks with two suspended.

item.title: Can British PM Keir Starmer survive the Mandelson scandal?
item.description: Sarah Ferguson presents Australia's premier daily current affairs program, delivering agenda-setting public affairs journalism and interviews that hold the powerful to account. Plus political analysis from Laura Tingle.

item.title: Final day of inquest into death of Toko-Harieta Maki hears from top police, child safety representatives
item.description: A coronial inquest hears that Northern Territory authorities assessed that a father posed "no risk" to his child, despite allegations he repeatedly abused his partner and shook the baby.

item.title: Four 'finfluencers' suspected of giving unlawful advice hit with warning notices amid regulator crackdown
item.description: The warning notice to the so-called "finfluencers" relates to suspected provision of unlicensed financial advice, including promoting claims of guaranteed returns.

item.title: Viva Energy boss defends fuel price surge, says more refineries needed for secure supply
item.description: The Viva Energy refinery provides about 10 per cent of Australia's fuel. Its chief executive, Scott Wyatt, chats to Alan Kohler about the company's future in the wake of the Geelong fire.

item.title: Four charts that paint a worrying picture for global fertiliser prices
item.description: Farmers around the world are going to be paying more for one of their most important inputs, fertiliser. Here are four charts that explain why.

item.title: NDIS cuts leave participants concerned about what lies ahead
item.description: NDIS participants set up their lives around the scheme's rules. But now the rules are changing and they fear what lies ahead.

item.title: NDIS fraud crackdown will flip rules on their head for 'let it rip' provider market
item.description: NDIS Minister Mark Butler will end the practice of handwritten invoices, providers without qualifications and a "let it rip" market of profiteering operators. But the sector warns changes must be navigated carefully.

item.title: Baby boomers beware: Labor is reading the room on generational inequity
item.description: The Albanese government is reading the room when it comes to intergenerational equity ahead of the May 12 budget.

item.title: Britain bracing for prospect of sixth PM in seven years, as some say Keir Starmer's leadership 'now terminal'
item.description: Sir Keir Starmer, who led Labour to a landslide general election victory in July 2024, has become so unpopular that even some colleagues are framing his demise as a case of not if, but when.

item.title: Power price warning as proposed capital gains tax changes create 'uncertainty' for foreign investors
item.description: The treasurer says changes to capital gains tax for foreign investors is a clarification, but experts say it could dampen investment.

item.title: Weekly news quiz: Trump's eleventh-hour announcement, a disabled bird and a speedy humanoid robot
item.description: It's Friday, and that means it's time to test your news knowledge from the past seven days. Were you paying attention this week?

item.title: Today in history, April 24: Jane Fonda's important place in fitness industry thanks to workout video
item.description: In the 1980s, the fitness industry as we know it was just getting started. One of the most important people in developing the way we exercise today was Jane Fonda, who released a best-selling aerobics video in 1982.

item.title: Matildas goalkeeper Chloe Lincoln on finding her voice and commanding her idols
item.description: The A-League Women finals kick off this weekend, with Brisbane to make its first appearance in five years.

item.title: These fishermen in the South China Sea get paid for their time on water — not to fish
item.description: Off China's southern coast, hundreds of fishing boats sit idle. The crews aren't there to fish, but rather to "maintain presence" as part of a shadowy, state-funded fleet.

item.title: Wallaroos ready to face New Zealand in Anzac Day Test after wild weather on United States tour
item.description: Having survived the threat of tornadoes during their US tour, the Wallaroos are enjoying a distraction-free build-up to their Anzac Day Test against New Zealand.

item.title: When is the Anzac Day public holiday? Which states have Monday off?
item.description: Anzac Day is observed on April 25 every year. This year that date falls on a Saturday, but that doesn't mean a long weekend for everyone.

item.title: Tasmania Police sergeant used dispatch system in 'relentless' monitoring of wife in family violence matter
item.description: Text messages read in court set out how a Tasmania Police officer monitored his wife's movements, including via an internal dispatching system, as part of a 15-month family violence matter.

item.title: US Department of Justice watchdog announces Epstein files audit
item.description: The audit follows months of controversy over censorship, missed deadlines and the publication of survivors' details.

item.title: Adelaide Festival appoints new Writers' Week director in wake of crisis
item.description: The new chair of Adelaide Writers' Week has sent a statement to would-be critics, quipping that Pauline Hanson would be worthy of inclusion on a literary line-up if the One Nation leader were "secretly writing Australia's next best crime novel".

item.title: Fuel prices in Australia: how rising costs are forcing Australians to change their habits
item.description: Petrol prices are reshaping Australian life in ways big and small — from a grandmother's isolation to a farmer's future.

item.title: Iran daily briefing: Israel, Lebanon peace talks, Trump's 'shoot and kill' order, and what else to know
item.description: Talks in Washington are set against a backdrop of decades-long disputes and deep mistrust, but at least they're happening.

item.title: Adam Walton earns big Madrid Open victory, Alexei Popyrin's 2026 season struggles continue
item.description: Queenslander Adam Walton has enjoyed a fine win at the Madrid Open to arrest a miserable sequence of Aussie losses at the clay-court event.

item.title: Rockhampton landmark The Criterion Hotel for sale for first time in 35 years
item.description: From hosting royalty to stars popping in for a steak, the hotel has a spooky past with tales of a jilted lover.

item.title: Gas giants get resource for free? That's 'tosh', says King — as it happened
item.description: Resources Minister Madeleine King has taken aim at those who say gas giants are obtaining the commodity for free, saying she's "mystified as to how they get away with such tosh".

item.title: Regional providers, families fear they will be hit hardest by cuts to NDIS
item.description: People living with disability in regional areas already face extra hurdles in accessing support and families fear changes to the NDIS could have irreversible consequences.

item.title: Alison didn't recognise the symptoms of her stroke. She wants to stop others making the same mistake
item.description: When Alison Reidy, then 38, woke in terrible pain, she had no idea she was having a stroke. Now she's trying to improve stroke awareness in the Northern Territory.

item.title: Appeal board says homophobic and racist language commonplace in AFL due to 'highly competitive' nature of the game
item.description: The appeal board in the Lance Collard homophobia case says homophobic and racist language is commonplace in the AFL due to the "highly competitive" nature of the game.

item.title: ASX 200 down after Wall Street losses, oil prices spike again — as it happened
item.description: Australia's ASX 200 has ended the week on a disappointing note, although the market clawed back most of its earlier losses to finish less than 0.1 per cent down for the day. Meanwhile, oil prices are rising again with Brent crude trading above $US105 a barrel. Look back on day's events with the ABC News markets blog.

item.title: Victoria Cross recipient Keith Payne calls for federal government to step up veteran support
item.description: One of Australia's most decorated soldiers has called for a national rethink on the care of veterans and their families.

item.title: Prince Harry urges Putin to 'stop this war' on visit to Ukraine
item.description: Prince Harry makes a surprise visit to Kyiv, urging Russia's President Vladimir Putin to end the war and calling on US President Donald Trump to show leadership to help resolve the conflict in Ukraine.

item.title: Is it ever OK to use someone else's toothbrush?
item.description: About half of us have shared a toothbrush — usually between spouses, or parents and children. We asked the experts if it's a good idea, even in an emergency.

item.title: The 10 best books out in April, featuring Patrick Radden Keefe and Caro Claire Burke
item.description: This month's top picks include new works from Steve Toltz and Colm Tóibín, a satire inspired by Yellowface and an investigation into the death of a London teen who masqueraded as the son of a Russian oligarch.

item.title: Two people arrested after $13m drug seizure on Hume Highway
item.description: A man and woman in their 20s have appeared in court after allegedly being found with 33 kilograms of methylamphetamine.

item.title: Mapping the most critical shipping lanes in the world
item.description: Oil prices have spiked since the Strait of Hormuz was closed. With about 90 per cent of all traded goods shipped by sea, what are the other key shipping lanes in the world?

item.title: Whale rescuers prepare for increased entanglements during annual migration
item.description: As thousands of whales begin their annual northern migration up the east coast of Australia, teams of specialist rescuers are preparing to help them along the way.

item.title: Sick kangaroos isolated by urban growth on NSW Mid North Coast
item.description: Kangaroos lying on a NSW mid north coast headland may be a "selfie" dream for tourists, but some are becoming weak from malnutrition.

item.title: Italy dismisses Trump official's 'shameful' idea to replace Iran at football World Cup
item.description: Italian officials say they are not interested in replacing Iran at the FIFA World Cup, after the idea was raised by a Trump ally.

item.title: Resilient Queensland bush community keeps Eidsvold Cattle Drive alive
item.description: A dedicated band of volunteers is keeping a cattle drive tradition alive to raise money for vital health services.

item.title: US soldier charged with making $560,000 on Nicolás Maduro removal with Polymarket bets
item.description: In the weeks leading up to Venezuelan leader Nicolás Maduro's January 3 capture, Gannon Ken Van Dyke used sensitive classified information to make wagers on prediction market Polymarket.

item.title: Canberra United in search of a lifeline on the eve of elimination finals
item.description: Canberra United is once again without an owner and facing an uncertain future, in a tale all too familiar for players and fans of the A-League's only stand-alone women's team.

item.title: Yamatji Vietnam veteran Graham Taylor remains brothers in arms with platoon mates from 1971
item.description: Yamatji man Graham Taylor was just 19 when he shipped off to Vietnam. More than 50 years on, the brotherhood forged in the jungle of Vietnam remains solid.

item.title: Tobacco ban in the UK could create a smoke-free generation
item.description: Anyone born after 2008 will be banned from buying cigarettes or vapes for life in the UK when a new bill becomes law.

item.title: Man who helped fellow runner across Boston Marathon finish line says it was natural to help
item.description: Aaron Beggs, who helped fellow runner Ajay Haridasse across the Boston Marathon finish line, says "the natural instinct was just to go and pick him up".

item.title: Dozen more Aboriginal burial sites detected amid construction on Rottnest Island
item.description: Authorities believe there may be a dozen more unmarked Aboriginal burial sites near where human remains were found on Western Australia's tourist haven Rottnest Island earlier this month.

item.title: 100-year-old bugler prepares for Anzac Day ceremonies in regional Victoria town
item.description: A Boort centenarian prepares to sound his bugle this Anzac Day at local schools and the RSL.

item.title: Newmont's Cadia gold mine aims to fix earthquake damage in five weeks
item.description: Underground operations at Newmont's Cadia gold mine are set to resume in five week's time, after a record earthquake that shook Central West NSW.

item.title: Horse trainer wants Australia to remember light horsemen, their 'amazing' companions this Anzac Day
item.description: Every year on Anzac Day, for more than two decades, Janette Wilson saddles up her Waler horse and leads the procession for the Darwin march, representing the light horsemen and their companions who served in World War I.

item.title: Perth woman warns about pedicure safety after severe infection almost costs her a toe
item.description: Kyla Willcox says she almost lost her toe from a potentially fatal infection after having a pedicure at a Perth nail salon.

item.title: Failing on social mobility 'is inefficiency with a human face'
item.description: Is reducing inequality and increasing social mobility one of Australia's best paths to improving our lacklustre economic productivity performance?

item.title: Kyle Sandilands returns to court over termination of ARN Media contract
item.description: Kyle Sandilands and Jackie 'O' Henderson have separately launched legal proceedings following the termination of their $100 million contracts.

item.title: Camper describes moment 'brazen' dingo attacked a child in outback WA
item.description: Kristen Bennetts says a dingo came dangerously close to her family during a night camping at Karijini National Park. Then, she heard a "horrendous scream".

item.title: Conductor, composer and pianist Michael Tilson Thomas, known for work with San Francisco Symphony, dies aged 81
item.description: The celebrated conductor led the San Francisco Symphony for 25 years, and co-founded the New World Symphony.

item.title: Defamation trial shown messages between Rebel Wilson and film producer
item.description: A Sydney court is shown a text message exchange between Hollywood star Rebel Wilson and a movie producer over a bathroom incident involving a young actor.

item.title: AFL 'specifically rejects' appeal board chair's comments regarding homophobic slurs after Lance Collard verdict
item.description: Both the AFL and the AFL Players' Association hit back at suggestions from the appeals board that slurs are commonplace among players in the sport.

item.title: Gas giant Woodside says additional tax on gas exports would kill projects at Senate inquiry
item.description: Oil and gas giant Woodside denies its business model is "weak" while arguing that paying a proposed flat tax on gas exports would kill the company's projects.

item.title: Why the gas tax momentum is building as Australia looks to properly cash in on its abundant natural resources
item.description: Gas giants are breathing a sigh of relief after Prime Minister Anthony Albanese ruled out a 25 per cent tax on exports in next month's budget. But for how long will the federal government resist something that one leading expert says is in our "national interest"?

item.title: Berri Barmera councillor convicted of drink driving able to keep licence until after Anzac Day
item.description: Berri Barmera Councillor Ian Schlein will hand in his licence several days after a regional court convicted him of drink driving, to allow him to attend an Anzac Day parade.

item.title: Man charged with murder of football hero's brother Brendan Breen
item.description: Victoria Police charge a Colac man with murder as part of its investigation into the 2012 suspicious disappearance of St Kilda football champion Barry Breen's brother, Brendan Breen.

item.title: Injury-decimated Broncos score spectacular tries to heap more pain on Dogs
item.description: Injury-hit Brisbane stands tall at a wet and wild Lang Park, scoring two remarkable tries in a 32-12 defeat of Canterbury-Bankstown.

item.title: India wants to redraw its electoral map for the first time in decades, but could this marginalise its southern states?
item.description: India's government proposed a plan to get more women into parliament but it came with one major condition.

item.title: Markets 'hanging on' to a possible Iran war resolution
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Oyster growing areas closed again on the Yorke Peninsula due to toxic phytoplankton
item.description: Oyster growing areas on the Yorke Peninsula are ordered to close due to the detection of a toxic phytoplankton for the second time this year.

item.title: Norway and Türkiye follow Australia with youth social media bans
item.description: Several European nations seek to rein in children's use of social media after Australia introduced its ban last December.

item.title: NSW star Kezie Apps says Women's State of Origin timing 'not ideal' after six-month lay-off for players
item.description: Kezie Apps is one of a number of Origin stars who will play her first match in a matter of months when she represents the Blues, and she doesn't believe the lay-off is the best preparation.

item.title: Massive reduction in road safety offences thanks to AI-assisted cameras, WA government says
item.description: The cameras have handed out around 184,000 infringements since they started issuing fines in October last year, according to the WA government.

item.title: Woman chooses to go blind in one eye after delays in care at Modbury Hospital
item.description: A 47-year-old woman receiving care at a public eye clinic in South Australia says her experience has been so poor she is choosing to go blind in one eye.

item.title: Bicoloured weevils feeding on cotton flowers
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Pickett takes mark of the year contender in Demon's stunning outing
item.description: Melbourne eases to a 54-point over Richmond at the MCG, as the hapless Tigers remain the competition's only side without a win in 2026. Follow live.

item.title: Family of litter pickers celebrate highway clean up with vanilla slice
item.description: A family has spent the last ten months cleaning litter along a 43-kilometre stretch of highway on WA's south coast. Today they finally reached their destination — a country bakery where they enjoyed their just desserts.

item.title: Thousands gather for journalist's funeral
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Former high-profile NT doctor committed to stand trial on multiple sexual offences, including rape
item.description: A former high-profile Northern Territory doctor charged with multiple sexual offences, including rape, has been committed to stand trial later this year.

item.title: Australians with ISIS links leave Al-Roj camp in Syria to attempt journey to Australia
item.description: A group of Australian families with ties to former Islamic State fighters have begun another attempt to travel home to Australia after spending years stranded in a Syrian refugee camp.

item.title: US considers reviewing Falkland Islands sovereignty, suspending Spain from NATO over Iran war disapproval
item.description: The email suggests the US may review the UK's claim to the Falkland Islands and Spain's NATO membership as punishment over their stance on the Iran war.

item.title: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu treated for prostate cancer
item.description: Benjamin Netanyahu said a malignant tumour was discovered during a routine check-up and he has since undergone "targeted treatment".

item.title: Is Something For Kate frontman Paul Dempsey Australia's best cover artist?
item.description: From Taylor Swift to Cher, Paul Dempsey's cover versions have been lauded. But does he have a secret weapon to making the perfect cover?

item.title: ABC 50/50 quiz: This week we're testing your knowledge of The Shawshank Redemption, hair metal and Bugs Bunny
item.description: Every question has only two options: one is right, and the other is wrong. You have an even chance of guessing correctly each time.

item.title: Alphabet Lane asks how can a relationship survive a rural tree change
item.description: Director James Litchfield brings his experience growing up in rural Australia to his debut feature film, Alphabet Lane.

item.title: World War II veterans meet for first time eight decades after Borneo service
item.description: Two of Australia's oldest veterans served in the same place at the same time during World War II, and after the fighting, both headed home to resume their lives in SA — but the 108-year-old and the 103-year-old had never met until recently.

item.title: With Mojtaba Khamenei a 'symbolic sovereign', who is running Iran?
item.description: After a wave of assassinations targeting top Iranian officials and a supreme leader who has not been seen in public since the war began, a key question has emerged: who is really running Iran?

item.title: How the NDIS grew to four times the size that was expected
item.description: An analysis of price forecasts in previous budgets reveals the NDIS was on track to cost more than four times what was initially expected.

item.title: Virginia Giuffre's legacy and the year that followed the Epstein files release
item.description: Virginia Giuffre didn't live to witness the full impact of the truths she brought forward, but the arrests, resignations and the unravelling of reputations came in the wake of her decision to speak.

item.title: Israel's killing of journalists in Lebanon shows a lack of guardrails on its actions
item.description: According to the Committee to Protect Journalists, the Israel Defense Forces have committed more targeted killings of journalists than any other government's military since 1992.

item.title: The outcome of the Farrer by-election could be a watershed moment in Australian politics
item.description: The Coalition's preference deals in Farrer could help fracture the Liberal Party's moderate base and invigorate dissatisfied voters.

item.title: James Valentine loved us in our most ridiculous moments
item.description: As an aspiring broadcaster, I covertly watched everything James Valentine did on air. He taught me how in the absurd minutia of our lives, we reveal ourselves to each other.

item.title: Why Anzac Day Test between Australia and New Zealand means so much to Wallaroos star Piper Duck
item.description: Any time Piper Duck pulls on the Australian jersey is a special moment, but playing in the historic Anzac Day Test holds even greater significance for the Wallaroos back-rower.

item.title: In search of the missing artist
item.description: An Australian gallery has been selling sculptures as the work of an "internationally acclaimed" French artist. But the Paris backstory doesn’t stack up.

item.title: Penny the donkey marches in Mount Gambier's Anzac Day parade to honour animals that serve in war
item.description: Penny the donkey, adorned in bright purple poppies, is spreading the Anzac Day legacy not just in Australia, but also in Cheshire in England.

item.title: Prime Minister joins thousands gathered at Australian War Memorial to commemorate Anzac Day
item.description: Prime Minister Anthony Albanese and New Zealand High Commissioner Andrew Needs are among those to gather in Canberra for the 111th anniversary of the Gallipoli landings.

item.title: Carlos Alcaraz out of French Open with wrist injury, Jannik Sinner urges him not to rush back
item.description: Two-time defending champion Carlos Alcaraz will not be able to defend his titles at Roland Garros due to a wrist injury that has friend and rival Jannik Sinner concerned.

item.title: Tasmanian musician's Anzac biscuit-tin guitars sold for veteran mental health charity
item.description: A Tasmanian guitar maker will donate Anzac biscuit-tin guitars to his local RSL to raise money for veteran mental health charity.

item.title: Young Indigenous artist uses work to explore personal stories, experiences in care system
item.description: An Indigenous teenager is already making a name for herself as an artist, using her work to explore her identity and experiences in the care system.

item.title: The art of apologising is being weaponised and social media might be to blame
item.description: There's almost something weaponised about the contemporary apology, and perversely the social media culture that aims to teach self-awareness and accountability might just be responsible for it.

item.title: Alex de Minaur beaten by Rafael Jodar in second round of Madrid Masters
item.description: Alex de Minaur loses another early-round match at the Madrid Masters, going down to local teen Rafael Jodar in straight sets as his clay court season struggles.

item.title: Australians with ISIS links attempt to return home from Syria
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Aboriginal rock art discovered in remote Cape York during burn planning
item.description: Rangers have discovered an undocumented rock art site while conducting planned burns around existing sites.

item.title: Bushfire survivors build fire-resilient house into Buchan hillside
item.description: During the Black Summer bushfires, the Grahams watched their house burn from a peephole in a fire bunker. When they decided to rebuild, they created the most fire-resilient home possible. Here's how they did it.

item.title: Indigenous solar projects in Borroloola and Santa Teresa to tackle diesel shortage concerns and halve power bills
item.description: Two remote Indigenous communities have attracted a combined $11 million in funding for solutions to tackle their fuel security and cost-of-living crises.

item.title: Blocking high to control eastern Australia's weather next week
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Australia's weather forecasts are suddenly identical each day
item.description: Darwin to Melbourne, and everywhere in between, the seven-day forecasts reveal a monotonous similarity.

item.title: Voluntary assisted dying advocates credit awareness raised by James Valentine and his family
item.description: For someone who revelled in celebrating humanity, one of James Valentine's final wishes was to make it publicly known his choice to access NSW's VAD laws.

item.title: Retired anti-whaling vessel MV Steve Irwin sets sail for conservation comeback
item.description: The MV Steve Irwin anti-whaling vessel returns to marine conservation, restored to its former glory, after more than seven years out of action.

item.title: Endangered mountain pygmy possum numbers climb at Secret Creek Sanctuary breeding program
item.description: Trevor Evans has been breeding the endangered mountain pygmy possum to better deal with the effects of climate change. The population at his sanctuary has grown rapidly in the process.

item.title: Trump administration to add firing squad, electrocution, gas asphyxiation to federal execution methods
item.description: The US is one of very few Western nations still using the death penalty, although public support for capital punishment has declined among Americans.

item.title: Farmers in WA food bowl region take $25 million cyclone hit
item.description: Growers across Western Australia's Gascoyne take a huge financial hit after four natural disasters in the first four months of 2026.

item.title: US Justice Department drops criminal investigation of Federal Reserve chairman Jerome Powell
item.description: The move could lead to a swift confirmation vote by the Senate for Kevin Warsh, a former top Federal Reserve official whom President Donald Trump nominated in January to replace Mr Powell.

item.title: Booing during Welcome to Country at Melbourne, Sydney and Perth Anzac Day services draws condemnation
item.description: Indigenous elders face racist booing from crowd members at dawn services in Melbourne, Sydney and Perth, with one man arrested and charged over his alleged involvement and others issued with move-on orders.

item.title: This easy 'hug in a bowl' chicken stew is perfect for midweek dinners - ABC News
item.description: "It's a shortcut to that feeling that you get when you've slow cooked something all Sunday, but without that time or labour."

item.title: US boosts Iran war assets with third aircraft carrier USS George HW Bush
item.description: USS George HW Bush has arrived in the Middle East, bringing thousands more American troops and advanced fighter jets to the region.

item.title: Cox's goal celebration sparks scuffle as Freo holds off Carlton comeback
item.description: Mason Cox gets in the face of Ben Ainsworth after kicking a fourth-quarter goal, resulting in some fireworks as Fremantle wins its sixth consecutive match.

item.title: Nelly Korda streaks ahead in Chevron Championship second round as Hannah Green sneaks under the cut
item.description: The Australian charge is struggling at the Chevron Championship in Texas, where world number two Nelly Korda is on fire after two rounds.

item.title: What Anzac Day means to some of the two million Australians who have served since Gallipoli
item.description: Australians taking part in Anzac Day commemorations share stories of sacrifice and bravery, and what today means to them.

item.title: AFL sacks appeals board chair Will Houghton after Lance Collard case
item.description: Will Houghton KC is sacked as the chair of the AFL appeals board after the body's widely criticised statement and decision in the case of St Kilda player Lance Collard.

item.title: Three men arrested after alleged arson attack in Melbourne CBD
item.description: Police were led on a foot-chase by alleged arsonists after a bar was set alight in ACDC lane early on Saturday morning.

item.title: Cotton industry 'alert but not alarmed' by increasing numbers of bicoloured weevils in NT crops
item.description: Farmers and researchers notice a rise in bicoloured weevils feeding on cotton crops in the Northern Territory.

item.title: Girls consider dropping sports due to periods, new research shows
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: NSW childcare worker charged with allegedly slapping two boys in Sydney's south
item.description: A 25-year-old man was arrested at an address in Belmore on Saturday morning.

item.title: How Henry the dachshund puppy survived three months lost in Perth's southern suburbs
item.description: A dachshund puppy is reunited with its owner after being lost for almost three months, in an adventure spanning multiple suburbs.

item.title: Captain Grant bristles at 'silly question' as Storm sink to historic low
item.description: Melbourne captain Harry Grant has defended coach Craig Bellamy after the Storm sank to a historic low following a 48-6 belting at the hands of South Sydney.

item.title: Beaches at Sydney's Royal National Park close after dead whale brings 'significant' shark activity
item.description: Several popular beaches in Sydney's Royal National Park are closed due to increased shark activity after a large whale carcass washes up on a rock platform.

item.title: Black Ferns outclass Wallaroos in Anzac Day Test
item.description: The Black Ferns produce a dominant performance to defeat the Wallaroos 40-5 in the first Anzac Day Test between the trans-Tasman rivals.

item.title: Australian Olympian Gordon Ingate dies aged 100
item.description: The Australian Olympian was still competing and winning into his 90s.

item.title: Donald Trump cancels Pakistan trip for US envoys as Middle East peace hopes fade
item.description: Donald Trump cancels a trip to Pakistan by US representatives Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner, with talks aimed at ending the two-month Iran war at a standstill as both Tehran and Washington show little willingness to soften their terms.

item.title: Today in history, April 26: Chernobyl's nuclear disaster, 40 years on
item.description: Nuclear meltdown in 1986 reshaped ecosystems and human lives. Four decades on the legacy of the Chernobyl disaster is still unfolding.

item.title: Calling all word wizards. Guy Montgomery's here with your Sunday spelling quiz
item.description: Delighted by diagraphs or hyped for homophones? Step inside and showcase your spelling skills.

item.title: Ringo Starr brought country music to The Beatles — now he's getting back to it
item.description: Ringo's childhood love of classic country still influences the new music that The Beatles's drummer makes.

item.title: Booing at Anzac Day services shows lack of public awareness of racism in Australia
item.description: Jeers could be heard for more than a minute as Uncle Ray Minniecon spoke at yesterday's Anzac service in Sydney with similar scenes playing out in Melbourne and Perth.

item.title: Prince Harry and Meghan's Australia trip is a contrast to King Charles's US state visit
item.description: Little more than a week after Meghan and Harry's "quasi royal" tour in Australia, King Charles will be conducting the real thing in America, with much higher stakes.

item.title: Australia's lucrative drug market is fuelling a wave of narco subs crossing the Pacific
item.description: At least seven narco subs have been discovered in Pacific waters in recent years, signalling a troubling new reality.

item.title: Sex workers speak out about the HIV crisis in Papua New Guinea
item.description: Workers have told the ABC about the battle to stay safe as the Pacific nation battles a rising number of infections.

item.title: Former Treasury boss says Australians left with little to show from commodities boom
item.description: Former Treasury secretary Ken Henry says the under-taxed resources sector has hollowed out Australia's economy, and raising more revenue from the sale of LNG resources was in the national interest.

item.title: The grave adopters still caring for fallen Australian soldiers
item.description: In The Netherlands, a quiet commitment to honour the young soldiers who were buried thousands of kilometres from home has continued for 80 years.

item.title: NDIS cuts show when Labor will move, gas tax shows when it won't
item.description: Labor's radical transformation of the NDIS as it resists a growing chorus of support for a new gas tax exposes the limit of its willingness to act when given a social licence.

item.title: Trump says US envoys waste too much time travelling, cancels Pakistan peace trip — as it happened
item.description: The US president says there has been "too much time wasted on travelling" by his officials, repeating suggestions the Iranian leadership remains unsettled.

item.title: Did you know Australia has three flags? Red, white and blue
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Ensign restoration has WWI flag taking pride of place in Nowra museum
item.description: A century-old red Australian flag that once led young men on a 100-kilometre march to enlist for World War I now takes pride of place in a museum in Nowra.

item.title: Gippsland footballer with Guillain-Barré syndrome returns to field after induced coma
item.description: A Gippsland footballer who spent 15 days in an induced coma after contracting a rare nerve condition has made a remarkable return to the field.

item.title: Long-held bitumen standards waived after supply caught in Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Drivers could soon be travelling on roads built for US and European climates, as supply disruptions force governments to change the standard of bitumen used in Australia.

item.title: Dragons halfback Kade Reed to keep starting role, says interim coach Dean Young
item.description: Dean Young backs Kade Reed to remain St George Illawarra's halfback even after the Dragons slumped to a 62-16 thrashing in the 20-year-old's highly anticipated NRL debut.

item.title: Developer Assemble withdraws significant number of promised near-complete affordable homes
item.description: Victorian planning authorities will allow Assemble to significantly reduce the number of affordable homes offered at large developments in Melbourne's north by more than half.

item.title: Victoria's Moorabool River is struggling for water, but there is still life in it yet
item.description: A small fish that smells like freshly-cut cucumber could be akin to a canary in the coalmine for one of Victoria's most stressed rivers.

item.title: What happens to a car once it has been stolen and wrecked, and what if hazards are left inside?
item.description: 'Opportunistic' car thieves are leaving vehicles littered with syringes, weapons and even traps, and in 2025, 77 per cent of vehicles inspected by one contractor had signs of methylamphetamine used at levels harmful to health.

item.title: 'Tombstone tourists' keeping Australia's oldest surviving European cemetery alive in Parramatta
item.description: Nestled between apartment blocks and opposite an escape room, St John's Cemetery holds many unique stories from history.

item.title: Ollie Hollands a late out for Carlton after Elijah Hollands's struggles, but coach Michael Voss says he is 'in a good space'
item.description: Carlton coach Michael Voss says Elijah Hollands's younger brother Ollie is doing OK despite being a late out for the club's Anzac Day clash with Fremantle.

item.title: Predator-free farm helps endangered species thrive
item.description: A predator-proof fence at a Victorian farm is helping endangered species recover while proving conservation and agriculture can successfully co-exist.

item.title: Queensland's remote national park islands where you can volunteer and holiday for free
item.description: North West Island is one of five national parks where you can holiday in exchange for campground hosting duties.

item.title: Ancient, critically endangered 'sterile' plant King's lomatia found for second time in Tasmanian wilderness
item.description: A second population of an ancient tree, King's lomatia, has been found in the south-west of Tasmania. A scientist says the precise location must be kept secret, or risk being 'killed by love'.

item.title: Thirty years after losing his family in the Port Arthur massacre, Walter Mikac says gun control is still vital
item.description: On April 28, 1996, a gunman killed 35 people at Port Arthur. Walter Mikac's wife and two daughters were among them. Thirty years on, he says gun control is as important as ever.

item.title: Indigenous kids training for life in and out of the ring at youth development camp in Canberra
item.description: A Canberra Indigenous-led boxing camp is helping young fighters hone their skills for life inside and outside the ring in its first development camp, continuing a proud tradition of First Nations involvement in the sport.

item.title: Preparing food for future use with a pressure cooker
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Seed swaps, preserved food and community gardens as preppers brace for uncertain future
item.description: It may not be the end of the world, but these Australians are finding creative ways to beat the cost-of-living crisis and improve their food security in troubled times.

item.title: Indonesian farmers improve food security while protecting Australia from citrus disease
item.description: An Australian-funded project is educating Indonesian farmers about a devastating citrus disease that has spread to all major growing regions except Australia.

item.title: Police investigate suspected drive-by shooting, firebombing in Melbourne's south-east
item.description: Police are investigating multiple incidents overnight including a drive-by shooting, suspected firebombing and an allegedly violent attack on a tram.

item.title: FOI documents shed light on appointment of controversial NT Administrator David Connolly
item.description: Months before a furore over his social media posts, documents show the federal government completed background checks on controversial NT Administrator David Connolly.

item.title: Why Shaun Micallef wrote 'gothic romance novel' De'Ath Takes a Holiday
item.description: After Shaun Micallef finished up 11 years hosting comedy news show Mad as Hell in 2022, the comedian did something unexpected: he wrote a 'gothic romance novel'.

item.title: Nelly Korda ties Chevron Championship's 54-hole record despite late struggles in third round
item.description: World number two Nelly Korda ties a Chevron Championship record as she takes a five-stroke lead into the final round of the year's first women's golf major.

item.title: Melbourne on track to record its longest run of warm April days in more than a decade
item.description: A blocking high has put Melbourne on track for its longest run of warm April days in more than a decade.

item.title: Catalpa captain George Anthony's descendants share historic family heirlooms with Fremantle Prison
item.description: Captain George Anthony's family share historic documents detailing the life of the principled Quaker who was branded a pirate for his role in the Catalpa rescue.

item.title: Married couple retire in their 90s after running small town's only hardware store for decades
item.description: The Pickles have been running the only hardware store in Junee for six decades, but now they are handing over the keys.

item.title: Angus Taylor avoids backing ASIO amid moves by ISIS-linked families to return to Australia
item.description: Government action needs to be taken to prevent a group of Australian families with links to former ISIS fighters from re-entering the country, Opposition Leader Angus Taylor says.

item.title: Resources industry in the spotlight during Angus Taylor's first trip to WA as Opposition leader
item.description: This past week marks the first time the Liberal leader visited WA since he took the position, and during his trip west, both Taylor and Premier Cook were navigating the state's hot-button issues.

item.title: Donald Trump evacuated from White House correspondents' dinner after suspected shooter apprehended
item.description: US President Donald Trump is evacuated from the White House correspondents' dinner after a security incident.

item.title: Moment Trump escorted out of gala dinner after security scare
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Search underway for five-year-old girl reported missing from Alice Springs home
item.description: The girl was reported missing in the early hours of this morning when she could not be located at her Alice Springs home.

item.title: Live: Trump says shooting suspect was armed with multiple weapons before being stopped by Secret Service
item.description: A man armed ‌with multiple weapons ‌charged a ‌security checkpoint at the White ‌House Correspondents' ‌Dinner in Washington DC, ‌wounding a ‌law ‌enforcement officer, US President Donald Trump said. Follow live.

item.title: Live: Trump says shooting suspect was armed with multiple weapons before being stopped by Secret Service
item.description: A man armed ‌with multiple weapons ‌charged a ‌security checkpoint at the White ‌House Correspondents' ‌Dinner in Washington DC, ‌wounding a ‌law ‌enforcement officer, US President Donald Trump said. Follow live.

item.title: Dingoes confront campers in WA's north
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: 'Main offender' dingo euthanised after multiple attacks in Karijini National Park
item.description: A male dingo has been "humanely destroyed" after multiple attacks within days of each other at Western Australia's famous Karijini National Park.

item.title: Trump shielded by security detail during security incident
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Mother of woman who died at Bandyup Women's Prison questions if daughter had adequate care
item.description: Deaths in custody are on the rise and family members and advocates for a woman who died in a Perth prison say more needs to be done to keep inmates safe.

item.title: Victorian government offers 20pc car registration discount in cost-of-living measure
item.description: Motorists will have two months to claim a 20 per cent rebate on car registration as the government attempts to counter price rises at the petrol pump.

item.title: Love story: Bonding with my daughter was amazing, but it took time
item.description: Perinatal depression and anxiety meant I thought my daughter would be better off with a different mum. But with the right support, things got better.

item.title: Only one known single spyridium plant is left in the wild on the Coorong, but scientists are hunting for more
item.description: Only one known single spyridium plant is left in the wild, in crushed limestone on the side of a road. But scientists are hunting for more.

item.title: US attorney for DC speaks after incident at White House Correspondents' Dinner
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Journalist’s firsthand account of Trump's evacuation from correspondents’ dinner
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Live: Focused Saints creating headaches for Eagles
item.description: West Coast faces St Kilda as it looks to halt a three-match losing streak. Later, the Brisbane Lions host the Adelaide Crows, and GWS meets North Melbourne. Follow live.

item.title: Tasmanian coastal community of Dolphin Sands on bushfire watch again
item.description: Dolphin Sands community recently ravaged by bushfire is again on alert in Tasmania.

item.title: Man expects to nab Guinness World Record after running half-marathon on stilts
item.description: Santiago Vergara, 33, hopes to have nabbed a Guinness World Record after running the Ballarat half-marathon on stilts in a time of 2 hours and 34 minutes.

item.title: Trump posts security vision of suspect from correspondents’ dinner
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Economist turns guerilla groundskeeper to save Sydney heritage building The Priory
item.description: After he was forced to retire, Craig James couldn't ignore the knee-high grass and decaying paint job on an 1840s sandstone building down the road.

item.title: Live: NRL leading Panthers travel to Newcastle to face Knights
item.description: The Penrith Panthers can end round with the outright lead on the NRL ladder with victory over the Knights in Newcastle. Follow live.

item.title: US attorney for DC details charges laid against suspect
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Advocate calls for 'common sense' after fine threat for using accessible zone without permit
item.description: Disability advocate and wheelchair user Shane Hryhorec is calling for Adelaide Airport to use a common sense approach after being threatened with a fine for using an accessible pick-up zone without a permit.
